curriculum.subject: MATHEMATICS
curriculum.overallDescription: 6 units, 6 chapters, 0 topics, 0 subtopics, and 11 learning outcomes extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Identify reflexive, symmetric, transitive, and equivalence relations on a set.
Determine if a function is one-to-one (injective) or onto (surjective).
Analyze the composition of two given functions.
Translate word problems into linear programming formulations (constraints and objective function).
Identify feasible and infeasible regions for given constraints.
Solve Linear Programming Problems graphically for two variables with up to three non-trivial constraints.
Calculate conditional probabilities for dependent and independent events.
Apply the multiplication theorem of probability.
Determine if two events are independent.
Use the Total Probability Theorem to solve problems involving mutually exclusive partitions.
Apply Bayes' Theorem to find posterior probabilities.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations
Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions

practical_assessment.component: Activities performed by the student throughout the year and record keeping
Assessment of the activity performed during the year end test

competency_groups.title: Relations and Functions
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to understanding types of relations, equivalence classes, one-to-one and onto functions.
competencies: Identify reflexive, symmetric, transitive, and equivalence relations on a set.
Determine if a function is one-to-one (injective) or onto (surjective).
Analyze the composition of two given functions.

competency_groups.title: Inverse Trigonometric Functions
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to domains, ranges, principal value branches, and graphs of inverse trigonometric functions.
competencies: Define the domain and range of inverse trigonometric functions.
Identify the principal value branch for each inverse trigonometric function.
Sketch and interpret graphs of inverse trigonometric functions.

competency_groups.title: Matrices
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to matrix operations, properties, types of matrices, and invertibility.
competencies: Classify matrices based on order, equality, symmetry (symmetric/skew-symmetric), and other specific types.
Perform addition, multiplication by a scalar, and matrix multiplication with correct notation and rules.
Demonstrate the non-commutativity of matrix multiplication using examples.
Find the inverse of an invertible square matrix and prove its uniqueness if it exists.

competency_groups.title: Determinants
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to determinants of square matrices, minors, cofactors, and applications in solving linear equations.
competencies: Calculate the determinant of a square matrix up to order 3x3 using minors and co-factors.
Find the area of a triangle given its vertices using determinants.
Determine the adjoint and inverse of a square matrix.
Analyze the consistency, inconsistency, or number of solutions for systems of linear equations (2 or 3 variables) using matrices.

competency_groups.title: Continuity and Differentiability
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to continuity, differentiability rules (chain rule, implicit), derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions, exponential/logarithmic functions, parametric forms, and higher-order derivatives.
competencies: Test the continuity and differentiability of a function at a given point or over an interval.
Apply chain rule to differentiate composite functions.
Differentiate inverse trigonometric, logarithmic, exponential, implicit, and parametric functions.
Perform logarithmic differentiation for complex functional forms.
Compute second-order derivatives.

competency_groups.title: Applications of Derivatives
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to rate of change, increasing/decreasing functions, maxima/minima using first and second derivative tests.
competencies: Calculate the rate of change of quantities with respect to time or other variables.
Determine intervals where a function is strictly increasing or decreasing.
Find local maxima and minima using the First Derivative Test (geometric motivation).
Verify extrema conditions using the Second Derivative Test.

competency_groups.title: Integrals
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to integration as the inverse process, substitution, partial fractions, parts, and evaluation of specific integral forms.
competencies: Evaluate indefinite integrals using methods: substitution, partial fractions, and integration by parts.
Solve standard integral types involving quadratic expressions under square roots or in denominators (e.g., 1/(x^2+a^2), sqrt(a^2-x^2)).
Apply the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus to evaluate definite integrals.
Utilize basic properties of definite integrals for simplification.

competency_groups.title: Application of Integrals
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to finding areas under curves, lines, circles, parabolas, and ellipses.
competencies: Calculate the area bounded by simple curves (lines, circles, parabolas, ellipses) in standard form.

competency_groups.title: Differential Equations
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to order, degree, general/particular solutions, and solving specific types of differential equations.
competencies: Identify the order and degree of a given differential equation.
Formulate general and particular solutions for basic differential equations.
Solve first-order homogeneous differential equations using separation of variables.
Solve linear differential equations of the form dy/dx + py = q (where p, q are functions of x or constants) and dx/dy + px = q.

competency_groups.title: Vectors and Three-dimensional Geometry
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to vector algebra, scalar/cross products, direction cosines/ratios, lines in 3D space, skew lines, and shortest distance.
competencies: Differentiate between vectors and scalars; identify types of vectors (equal, unit, zero, parallel, collinear).
Calculate magnitude, direction cosines, and components of a vector.
Perform vector addition and scalar multiplication geometrically and algebraically.
Compute dot product and cross product properties and applications.
Derive Cartesian and vector equations for lines in 3D space.
Find the shortest distance between two skew lines.

competency_groups.title: Linear Programming
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to constraints, objective functions, feasible regions, and graphical optimization methods.
competencies: Translate word problems into linear programming formulations (constraints and objective function).
Identify feasible and infeasible regions for given constraints.
Solve Linear Programming Problems graphically for two variables with up to three non-trivial constraints.

competency_groups.title: Probability
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to conditional probability, multiplication theorem, independent events, total probability, and Bayes' theorem.
competencies: Calculate conditional probabilities for dependent and independent events.
Apply the multiplication theorem of probability.
Determine if two events are independent.
Use the Total Probability Theorem to solve problems involving mutually exclusive partitions.
Apply Bayes' Theorem to find posterior probabilities.
